methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $34M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

data.narrative:
Terrahaptix, a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Nathan Nwachuku and Maxwell Maduka, has raised $34M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers (2 public, 8 private) in the same sector and stage, including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Anduril Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $34M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $68M. Peer-median analysis examines 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $400M, though the range is wide at $150M–$29.0B, reflecting heterogeneity in the sector. The peer-median method implies $400M.

Triangulating these two approaches produces a valuation range of $58M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $234M. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively near total capital deployed; the peer-median approach reflects what similar-stage defense-tech companies command, but is subject to material uncertainty given the wide dispersion in comparable valuations. Key sensitivities include technical differentiation relative to peers, customer concentration, and regulatory pathway clarity—factors not fully captured in the comps set.
